preamble: The voiced palatal approximant [j], particularly when occurring in a cluster with a preceding stop, can undergo a process of fortition, devoicing, and assimilation to become the voiceless alveolo-palatal fricative [ɕ]. This transformation often involves the loss of the preceding stop and the fricativization of the palatal glide.
phoneticEffects: Fortition (approximant to fricative), Devoicing, Palatalization, Cluster Simplification

examples.note: Creation of new alveolo-palatal fricatives from labial stop + j clusters, without passing through an affricate stage.

sources: Jacques, G. (2011). A panchronic study of aspirated fricatives, with new evidence from Pumi. Lingua, 121(10), 1518-1538.
